_comment: Curated future events for the Year Clock (2026-2359). Three types: astronomy, fiction, scheduled. Sources cited for each. Generated and maintained manually; see generate_future_events.py for extending with Skyfield.

2029.text: Asteroid Apophis (340m wide) passes within 31,600 km of Earth — closer than many satellites, briefly visible to the naked eye. The closest approach of a large asteroid in recorded history.
2029.source: NASA/JPL Center for Near Earth Object Studies

2031.text: In Isaac Asimov's robot stories, the positronic brain becomes commercially viable, beginning the age of domestic robots.

2035.text: In the Will Smith film adaptation of Asimov's 'I, Robot', robots outnumber humans and a vast network called VIKI plots to control humanity for its own protection.

2038.text: Unix time overflows: 32-bit systems that store time as seconds since 1 January 1970 will roll over to a negative number on 19 January 2038 at 03:14:08 UTC — the 'Year 2038 problem'.

2040.text: Great Conjunction: Jupiter and Saturn align closely in the sky, appearing as a brilliant double-star. These rare meetings occur roughly every 20 years; the previous conjunction in 2020 was the closest since 1226.

2044.text: In Ernest Cline's 'Ready Player One', energy shortages and inequality have driven billions of people to spend their lives inside the OASIS virtual reality.

2045.text: Ray Kurzweil's predicted year of the Technological Singularity — the point at which artificial superintelligence surpasses human intelligence and accelerates beyond any predictable trajectory.

2049.text: In Blade Runner 2049, humanoid replicants are a common part of society. The Nexus-8 and later models have open-ended lifespans, and a rebellion is brewing.

2052.text: Jorgen Randers' 'Club of Rome' forecast: global population peaks at about 8 billion, climate change is not solved in time, and economic growth in rich nations has stalled.
2052.source: 2052: A Global Forecast for the Next Forty Years (Randers, 2012)

2057.text: In Arthur C. Clarke's '2061: Odyssey Three', the spaceship Universe is nearing Jupiter on a mission to Halley's Comet — four years before the actual perihelion.

2061.text: Halley's Comet returns to the inner solar system, reaching perihelion around July 28. Visible to the naked eye, this will be its 30th observed return since 240 BCE — when Chinese astronomers first recorded it.
Setting of Arthur C. Clarke's '2061: Odyssey Three': a crewed expedition lands on Halley's Comet and discovers that Europa's ocean harbours life.

2063.text: In Star Trek lore, Zefram Cochrane makes humanity's first warp flight on April 5, 2063, attracting a passing Vulcan survey ship and triggering First Contact.

2079.text: Great Conjunction: Jupiter and Saturn align once more. These events were considered omens by astrologers throughout history — the 1226 conjunction marked the height of Genghis Khan's empire.

2081.text: In Kurt Vonnegut's story 'Harrison Bergeron', total equality has been enforced by law: talented and intelligent people are handicapped so that no one is above average.

2099.text: In Marvel Comics, Miguel O'Hara becomes Spider-Man 2099, operating in a dystopian New York run by mega-corporations.

2112.text: In Rush's concept album '2112', the Solar Federation — ruled by the Priests of the Temples of Syrinx — has banned all music. A lone man rediscovers a guitar and is destroyed for it.

2117.text: Transit of Venus: for the first time since 2012, Venus passes directly across the face of the Sun — visible as a small black dot. These transits come in pairs 8 years apart, separated by over a century. The 1769 transit is why Captain Cook sailed to Tahiti.

2125.text: Second Transit of Venus in the 2117–2125 pair. After this, no human alive will ever see one — the next pair is not until 2247 and 2255.

2137.text: Halley's Comet returns again — its third appearance since 1986, completing another ~76-year orbit around the Sun.

2150.text: In Isaac Asimov's Foundation universe, the Galactic Empire is at its height — 20,000 inhabited worlds, trillions of citizens, and a bureaucracy so vast it has forgotten how to change.

2161.text: In the Star Trek universe, the United Federation of Planets is founded — a voluntary interstellar union born out of the Coalition of Planets formed after the Earth-Romulan War.

2213.text: Halley's Comet completes yet another orbit, returning to perihelion roughly 76 years after its previous visit in 2137.

2233.text: In the Star Trek universe, James Tiberius Kirk is born in Riverside, Iowa — on the day a Romulan ship from the future destroys the USS Kelvin.

2239.text: The Jewish calendar reaches year 6000 — in some traditions, the threshold of the Messianic Age. Rosh Hashana 6000 falls in September 2239.

2245.text: In Star Trek, the USS Enterprise NCC-1701 is launched from the San Francisco Fleet Yards — the most famous starship in the Federation's history.

2247.text: Transit of Venus: the first since 2125. Anyone alive for this transit has waited 122 years since the last pair.

2255.text: Second Transit of Venus in the 2247–2255 pair — completing a cycle that began with the famous 1761/1769 transits used by Edmond Halley to measure the distance from Earth to the Sun.

2263.text: The five-year mission of the USS Enterprise under Captain James T. Kirk begins — exploring strange new worlds, seeking out new life and civilisations.

2289.text: Halley's Comet returns once more — its fifth orbit since 1986, still faithfully tracing the same path it has followed for at least 2,000 years of recorded history.

2293.text: In Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country, the Klingon moon Praxis explodes. Kirk and the Enterprise crew negotiate the Khitomer Accords — ending decades of cold war between the Federation and the Klingon Empire.
2293.source: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country (film, 1991)

2355.text: Jean-Luc Picard graduates from Starfleet Academy. In nine years he will take command of the USS Enterprise NCC-1701-D.
